title: potentilla clevelandii var. clevelandii
intro: Potentilla clevelandii var. clevelandii, also known as Cleveland's cinquefoil, is a perennial plant species belonging to the rose family (Rosaceae), characterized by its fern-like foliage and clusters of small, fragrant flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, and its medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it useful for treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Potentilla clevelandii var. clevelandii include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. It can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and has also been used topically to treat skin conditions and wounds.
